steps.title: Identify vertical angles
steps.content: When two lines intersect at a point, they form two pairs of vertical angles—angles that are opposite each other. In the figure, $r°$ and $s°$ are vertical angles.

steps.title: Apply the Vertical Angles Theorem
steps.content: Vertical angles are always equal. Since $r°$ and $s°$ are vertical angles, we have $s = r$. Substituting $r = 68$ gives $s = 68$.
